signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + CESI Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5671 anchors the week's dominant G10 theme as the USD hits one-year highs following PMI beats. USD outperformance (CESI net +30) contrasts with NZD weakness despite a hawkish RBNZ bias. The 1.803% G10 spread and 150bp USD carry advantage. lower; however, NZD COT LF net short -22,454 vs RBNZ hike bias creates a structural short-squeeze risk if the July 8 meeting delivers a hawkish surprise. Retail 96% long positioning marks an extreme contrarian warning against chasing the move at these levels.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Positioning + Vol + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4205 testing multi-month highs as Fed hike bets rise and oil prices weigh. COT LF net short -67,668 (long% 23.4%) indicates a crowded institutional short being squeezed, while retail 95% short positioning confirms extreme contrarian upside pressure. 150bp USD carry advantage supports the bid. Volatility pricing shows complacency with IV at 11.5% vs HV30 6.2%, suggesting the options market is not yet fully pricing the extension risk.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Vol + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.56 holding above the critical 160.00 threshold where BoJ intervention risk historically escalates. Despite JPY 1W outperformance (+0.502%), the 275bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage keeps the pair bid. Options market is pricing significant protection with a 25d RR of -1.38 (put skew) and an elevated VRP of +4.8% (IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.4%). Institutional hedging for a JPY move is active even as spot remains elevated.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Vol + Real Rates
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1384 breaks below 1.1400 as hawkish Fed bets and PMI beats support the USD. COT LF net short -40,372 aligns with a restrictive US real rate (+1.48%) vs EUR (+0.02%). Options market shows elevated hedging demand with a VRP of +2.1% (IV 7.1% vs HV30 5.0%) and a -0.63 25d RR put skew, signaling institutional preference for downside protection as the pair tests one-year lows.
